name: Zero Trust Architecture
description: A Zero Trust architecture that eliminates implicit trust based on network location. Every access request is continuously verified regardless of origin, enforcing the principle of 'never trust, always verify.' Identity becomes the primary security perimeter, with real-time policy evaluation, micro-segmentation, and continuous posture assessment replacing traditional perimeter-based security. Aligned with NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust Architecture.

zones.name: untrusted-network
zones.description: All network segments are treated as untrusted, including the internal corporate network. There is no trusted network zone in Zero Trust. All users, devices, and services must authenticate and be authorized for every request regardless of network location.

zones.name: identity-plane
zones.description: The identity verification plane providing continuous authentication. Combines user identity (MFA, passwordless), device identity (certificates, MDM compliance), and context signals (location, time, risk score) to establish confidence levels for access decisions.

zones.name: policy-engine
zones.description: The centralized policy engine that makes real-time authorization decisions for every access request. Evaluates policies combining identity confidence, device posture, resource sensitivity, and environmental context. The most critical component in the architecture.

zones.name: workloads
zones.description: Protected workloads and resources that enforce access decisions from the policy engine. Each workload verifies authorization tokens, enforces micro-segmentation, and communicates with other workloads only through authenticated and encrypted channels (mTLS).

zones.name: data
zones.description: The protected data layer with attribute-based access controls tied to data classification. Every data access is authorized based on the requestor's identity, device posture, and the data's sensitivity level. Encryption is enforced at rest and in transit with centrally managed keys.

boundaries.name: Workloads to Data
controls: Encryption at rest with managed keys

mermaid_template:
flowchart TB
    subgraph untrusted["Untrusted Network (All Networks)"]
        remote[Remote User]
        office[Office User]
        byod[BYOD Device]
        branch[Branch Office]
    end
    subgraph identity["Identity Plane (Trusted)"]
        idp(Identity Provider)
        mfa(MFA Service)
        device_trust(Device Trust / MDM)
        ca(Certificate Authority)
    end
    subgraph policy["Policy Engine (Highly Trusted)"]
        pdp(Policy Decision Point)
        pep(Policy Enforcement Point)
        risk(Risk Scoring Engine)
        threat_intel(Threat Intelligence)
        siem(SIEM Integration)
    end
    subgraph workloads_zone["Workloads (Trusted)"]
        app1(Application A)
        app2(Application B)
        api1(Internal API)
        saas(SaaS App Proxy)
    end
    subgraph data_zone["Data (Highly Trusted)"]
        classified[(Classified Data)]
        kms(Key Management)
        dlp(DLP Engine)
        rights(Rights Management)
    end
    remote -->|Auth Request| idp
    office -->|Auth Request| idp
    byod -->|Auth Request| idp
    branch -->|Auth Request| idp
    idp --> mfa
    idp --> device_trust
    idp --> ca
    mfa -->|Identity + Context| pdp
    device_trust -->|Posture| pdp
    pdp --> risk
    risk --> threat_intel
    risk --> siem
    pdp -->|Allow/Deny + Token| pep
    pep -->|mTLS + Token| app1
    pep -->|mTLS + Token| app2
    pep -->|mTLS + Token| api1
    pep -->|mTLS + Token| saas
    app1 <-->|mTLS| app2
    app1 <-->|mTLS| api1
    app1 -->|ABAC| classified
    app2 -->|ABAC| classified
    classified --> kms
    classified --> dlp
    classified --> rights
